questions.answer: The data gate blocks and downstream checksums become stale.
questions.claim_boundary: No decision is valid until grain is restored.
questions.next_check: Rerun data audit, baseline and every downstream stage.
questions.question: What happens if aggregate keys stop being unique?

questions.answer: That would replace a frozen gate with a post-hoc selection rule.
questions.claim_boundary: Sensitivity describes dependence but does not move the gate.
questions.next_check: Predeclare a new threshold in a new project version.
questions.question: Why not lower the practical threshold after seeing the result?

questions.answer: No; observed priority may reflect baseline risk or support burden.
questions.claim_boundary: The project makes a descriptive ranking claim only.
questions.next_check: Use an identified experiment before any causal claim.
questions.question: Does high_touch ranking prove support outreach reduces churn?

questions.answer: Operational reliability is not known from this tiny profile.
questions.claim_boundary: The package is a reproducible handoff, not an SLA claim.
questions.next_check: Run an owned scheduled pilot with freshness and escalation tests.
questions.question: What if the weekly owner or freshness process is unavailable?

questions.answer: The predeclared capacity sensitivity keeps baseline selected.
questions.claim_boundary: The conclusion applies only to evaluated capacities.
questions.next_check: Recalculate utility for any new sustained capacity constraint.
questions.question: What if review capacity falls from four users to three?
